hero.heading: Care that doesn't
hero.subhead: When our office is closed for doctor visits, an on-call physician is still within reach — by phone or video.
hero.note: Our team will point you in the right direction — virtual visit, scheduling an appointment, or whatever you need.

access.heading: Still your
access.body1: When the office is closed for doctor visits — after hours on weekdays, on weekends, and on Tuesdays — you still have a way to reach care. You start with a call, and our staff helps you choose the right next step.
access.body2: Not a call center. Your concern reaches a nurse, and a physician, who care for you just as we would in the office.

steps.label: Start with a call
steps.body: Reach our office and a team member points you in the right direction — paperwork, the patient portal, or where to go for what you need.

steps.label: Talk to a nurse
steps.body: For medical questions, our nurses — through Privia — can advise on most concerns and help decide what comes next.

steps.label: A physician calls you back
steps.body: If you need a doctor, an on-call physician reaches you by phone or video and cares for you just as if your own doctor called. Virtual visits bill to your insurance.

access.costNote: Virtual visits are billed separately and may have a co-pay. Have your insurance card handy, and text or call us with any billing questions.
access.ctaNote: Our team will help direct you to the right place — virtual visit, an appointment, or whatever you need.

tuesday.heading: Open at the front desk,
tuesday.intro: On Tuesdays our office is open for administrative needs, but Dr. Stewart isn't seeing patients that day. Here's how to get what you need:

options.label: Need a form or paperwork?
options.body: Come by on Tuesday — we're open and here to help with forms, referrals, or anything administrative.

options.label: Need to see Dr. Stewart?
options.body: Give us a call and we'll do our best to find you the next available appointment. We can't promise same-day, but we'll get you in as soon as we can.

options.label: Need care today?
options.body: A physician is available by phone or video through Privia Virtual Clinic — no urgent care required.

tuesday.note: Some services may still be available in person on Tuesdays — call and our staff will help you choose what's right for you.

setup.heading: Two minutes of prep
setup.intro: Once your visit moves to video, a couple of minutes of prep keeps it smooth. The most common snag is a camera or microphone blocked by your device — here's how to clear it before you join.

mobile.label: On a Phone
steps: Download the Privia Health app (App Store or Google Play), or open your visit link in your mobile browser.
Sign in to your Privia account before the visit starts.
When your phone asks for camera and microphone access, tap Allow.
Use Wi-Fi if possible — video calls are more stable than cellular.
If the call drops, check your confirmation text or email for a rejoin link. Your doctor may also call you back directly.
mobile.permissionNote: Tap Allow — you'll see a prompt like this.

desktop.label: On a Computer
steps: Use Google Chrome if possible — it's Privia's recommended browser. Edge and Safari also work. Internet Explorer is not supported.
When your browser asks for camera and microphone access, click Allow.
If you see a camera icon with a line through it in the address bar, click it to change the permission to Allow.
Close Zoom, FaceTime, Teams, or any other app currently using your camera.
If the call drops, check your confirmation email or text for a rejoin link. Your doctor may also call you back directly.
desktop.permissionNote: Click Allow — you'll see a prompt like this.

items.label: Insurance & medications
items.body: Have your insurance card and a current medication list nearby before you join.

items.label: Stay charged
items.body: Plug in your device or confirm your battery is above 20% before the visit begins.

items.label: Strong connection
items.body: Privia requires at least 20 Mbps. Not sure? Test at speedtest.net before your visit. If Wi-Fi is slow, try moving closer to your router or switching to a wired connection.

items.label: Good lighting
items.body: Face a window or a lamp so the doctor can see you clearly. Avoid sitting with a bright light behind you.

items.label: Be ready when the call comes
items.body: Your doctor will call you when they're ready. Have your device open, camera and microphone allowed, and a quiet spot picked out before you expect the call.

items.label: Open a fresh window
items.body: Open your virtual visit in its own browser window and close extra tabs. Other open windows can compete for bandwidth and slow the connection.

helpCta.heading: We're a message
helpCta.body: If you run into technical difficulties or have questions before your virtual visit, text or call our office — we'll help you get connected.
